title: Capnoides streptocarpa
taxonomy: Capnoides streptocarpa belongs to the family Menispermaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants in the order Ranunculales. The species is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Capnoides streptocarpa is a climber with unique, elongated fruits. This plant is often found in the understory of tropical rainforests.

intro: Capnoides streptocarpa, also known as Capnoides, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Capnoides streptocarpa include iridoid glycosides, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Capnoides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to reduce pain and inflammation, and to support the immune system.
